use_case_name: Stop Transaction with a Master Pass

structured.name: Stop Transaction with a Master Pass
structured.objective:
Enable stopping of transactions by use of a Master Pass (for example for: Law Enforcement
officials).
structured.description:
This use case covers how somebody with a Master Pass (User) can stop (selected) ongoing
transactions, so the cable becomes unlocked. This Master Pass can be configured in:
MasterPassGroupId.
scenario: 1. The User (Law Enforcement official) presents his IdToken at the Charging Station.
2. The Charging Station sends AuthorizeRequest to the CSMS to request authorization.

3. Upon receipt of AuthorizeRequest, the CSMS responds with AuthorizeResponse. This response
message contains a GroupId that equals the value of the Configuration Variable
MasterPassGroupId and the idToken is valid.
4a. If the Charging Station has a UI, then the Charging Station "Shows" the Master Pass UI.
5a. The user selects which transactions to stop.
6a. The Charging Station stops the selected transaction(s) AND sends a
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, stopReason = MasterPass) to the CSMS for every
stopped transaction.
7a. Upon receipt of TransactionEventRequest the CSMS responds with
TransactionEventResponse.
4b. If the Charging Station does NOT have a UI, then the Charging Station stops all transactions
AND sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, stopReason = MasterPass) to the
CSMS for every stopped transaction.
5b. Upon receipt of TransactionEventRequest the CSMS responds with
TransactionEventResponse.
structured.prerequisites:
Ongoing Transaction(s)
Configuration Variable: MasterPassGroupId set.
Users IdToken has groupId equal to the configured MasterPassGroupId.
structured.error_handling:
When the user does not make a selection before an acceptable timeout, the Charging Station
SHALL go back to normal operation.
structured.remarks:
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for stop transaction being configured as follows.
TxStopPoint: Authorized, PowerPathClosed, EnergyTransfer
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might stop at another
moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details see the
use case: E06 - Stop Transaction options

requirements.precondition:
User presents an IdToken that has a groupId
equal to MasterPassGroupId AND
The Charging Station has a UI with input
capabilities.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL "show" the Master Pass UI to let
user select which transaction to stop.

requirements.precondition:
User presents an IdToken that has a groupId
equal to MasterPassGroupId AND the
Charging Station does NOT have a UI.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop all ongoing transactions as
described in use case E07.

requirements.precondition:
IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId
requirements.definition: SHALL NOT be allowed to start a transaction.

requirements.precondition:
IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId present in the
Authorization Cache.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY also allow authorization of "Master
Pass" tokens based on information in the Authorization Cache.

requirements.precondition:
IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId present in the Local
Authorization List.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY also allow authorization of "Master
Pass" tokens based on information in the Local Authorization
List.

Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction as described in use
case E07.

Charging Station SHALL set transactionInfo.stoppedReason =
MasterPass in TransactionEventRequest with eventType =
Ended.

chunks.content:
# C16 - Stop Transaction with a Master Pass

**Objective:** Enable stopping of transactions by use of a Master Pass (for example for: Law Enforcement
officials).

**Description:** This use case covers how somebody with a Master Pass (User) can stop (selected) ongoing
transactions, so the cable becomes unlocked. This Master Pass can be configured in:
MasterPassGroupId.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS, User

**Scenario:**
1. The User (Law Enforcement official) presents his IdToken at the Charging Station.
2. The Charging Station sends AuthorizeRequest to the CSMS to request authorization.
3. Upon receipt of AuthorizeRequest, the CSMS responds with AuthorizeResponse. This response
message contains a GroupId that equals the value of the Configuration Variable
MasterPassGroupId and the idToken is valid.
4a. If the Charging Station has a UI, then the Charging Station "Shows" the Master Pass UI.
5a. The user selects which transactions to stop.
6a. The Charging Station stops the selected transaction(s) AND sends a
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, stopReason = MasterPass) to the CSMS for every
stopped transaction.
7a. Upon receipt of TransactionEventRequest the CSMS responds with
TransactionEventResponse.
4b. If the Charging Station does NOT have a UI, then the Charging Station stops all transactions
AND sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, stopReason = MasterPass) to the
CSMS for every stopped transaction.
5b. Upon receipt of TransactionEventRequest the CSMS responds with
TransactionEventResponse.

**Prerequisites:** Ongoing Transaction(s)
Configuration Variable: MasterPassGroupId set.
Users IdToken has groupId equal to the configured MasterPassGroupId.

**Postconditions:** (Selected) transaction(s) stopped.

**Error handling:** When the user does not make a selection before an acceptable timeout, the Charging Station
SHALL go back to normal operation.

**Remarks:** The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for stop transaction being configured as follows.
TxStopPoint: Authorized, PowerPathClosed, EnergyTransfer
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might stop at another
moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details see the
use case: E06 - Stop Transaction options

metadata.heading: C16 - Stop Transaction with a Master Pass
metadata.use_case_name: Stop Transaction with a Master Pass

chunks.content:
# C16 - Requirements

**C16.FR.01**
Precondition: User presents an IdToken that has a groupId
equal to MasterPassGroupId AND
The Charging Station has a UI with input
capabilities.
The Charging Station SHALL "show" the Master Pass UI to let
user select which transaction to stop.

**C16.FR.02**
Precondition: User presents an IdToken that has a groupId
equal to MasterPassGroupId AND the
Charging Station does NOT have a UI.
The Charging Station SHALL stop all ongoing transactions as
described in use case E07.

**C16.FR.03**
Precondition: IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId
SHALL NOT be allowed to start a transaction.

**C16.FR.04**
Precondition: IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId present in the
Authorization Cache.
The Charging Station MAY also allow authorization of "Master
Pass" tokens based on information in the Authorization Cache.

**C16.FR.05**
Precondition: IdTokens that have a groupId equal to
MasterPassGroupId present in the Local
Authorization List.
The Charging Station MAY also allow authorization of "Master
Pass" tokens based on information in the Local Authorization
List.

**C16.FR.07**
Precondition: C16.FR.01
Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction as described in use
case E07.

**C16.FR.08**
Precondition: C16.FR.02 OR C16.FR.07
Charging Station SHALL set transactionInfo.stoppedReason =
MasterPass in TransactionEventRequest with eventType =
Ended.

metadata.heading: C16 - Requirements
metadata.use_case_name: Stop Transaction with a Master Pass
